title: Is it common for repeaters not to

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: Perhaps. Is that a console command? I'm not yet that familiar with how to debug/interrogate the network
messages.content_html: Perhaps. Is that a console command? I'm not yet that familiar with how to debug/interrogate the network

embeds_html:
MeshCore CZ
MeshCore: Trasování cesty

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: Returning to the original question though: are a lot of repeaters non-advertising?
messages.content_html: Returning to the original question though: are a lot of repeaters non-advertising?

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: nope. would say it's pretty rare
messages.content_html: nope. would say it's pretty rare
messages.reply_html:
Seacourt
Returning to the original question though: are a lot of repeaters non-advertising?

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: Interesting. I wonder why I have such decent connectivity but no visible local neighbour
messages.content_html: Interesting. I wonder why I have such decent connectivity but no visible local neighbour

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: look if your repeater doesn't have low uptime
messages.content_html: look if your repeater doesn't have low uptime

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: That's all super helpful. Many thanks. So my repeater is a heltec V4 on mains power so it shouldn't be rebooting, but I could imagine the connection is intermittent. It's called 'Stanton Harcourt repeater'
messages.content_html: That's all super helpful. Many thanks. So my repeater is a heltec V4 on mains power so it shouldn't be rebooting, but I could imagine the connection is intermittent. It's called 'Stanton Harcourt repeater'

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: Oh. That's helpful. So all the public messages I've received all have unknown repeater as their penultimate hop before my repeater. Interesting you see the same
messages.content_html: Oh. That's helpful. So all the public messages I've received all have unknown repeater as their penultimate hop before my repeater. Interesting you see the same

messages.author: Seacourt
messages.text: Ok. Great. Many thanks. The obvious place for a repeater around here is a local hilltop, but the whole hill is owned by the university of Oxford and I can't imagine it being possible to get permission to install. So I had idly wondered if it might be possible to put a router up without adverts or lat/lon to be harder to detect.
messages.content_html: Ok. Great. Many thanks. The obvious place for a repeater around here is a local hilltop, but the whole hill is owned by the university of Oxford and I can't imagine it being possible to get permission to install. So I had idly wondered if it might be possible to put a router up without adverts or lat/lon to be harder to detect.
